questions.text: A physical quantity that has both magnitude and direction is called:
questions.explanation: Vectors are defined by both magnitude and direction.

questions.text: The angle for which the horizontal range of a projectile is maximum is:
questions.explanation: Range R = (u² sin 2θ)/g. R is max when sin 2θ = 1, i.e., 2θ = 90° or θ = 45°.

questions.text: The path followed by a projectile is called its:
questions.explanation: The curve traced by a projectile is a trajectory (specifically a parabola).

questions.text: Centripetal acceleration is directed:
questions.explanation: It always acts towards the center of the circular path.

questions.text: If two vectors are perpendicular, their dot product is:
The resultant of two vectors A and B acting at an angle θ is:
questions.explanation: Parallelogram law of vector addition formula.

questions.text: A vector whose magnitude is zero and has an arbitrary direction is:
questions.explanation: Also called a zero vector.

questions.text: The vertical component of velocity of a projectile at its highest point is:
questions.explanation: At the peak, vertical motion stops momentarily; only horizontal velocity (u cos θ) remains.

questions.text: In uniform circular motion, which of the following remains constant?
questions.explanation: Direction changes continuously, so velocity and acceleration change, but the magnitude of velocity (speed) is constant.

questions.text: The unit vector along the x-axis is denoted by:
questions.explanation: Standard notation for Cartesian axes: î(x), ĵ(y), k̂(z).

questions.text: A scalar product of a vector with itself is:
questions.explanation: A·A = |A||A| cos 0° = |A|².

questions.text: The cross product of two parallel vectors is:
questions.explanation: |AxB| = |A||B| sin 0° = 0.

questions.text: The time of flight of a projectile is:
questions.explanation: Time to rise + time to fall.

questions.text: Maximum height of a projectile is given by:
questions.explanation: Derived from kinematic equations for vertical motion.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a scalar quantity?
questions.explanation: Weight is a force (vector), while others are scalars.

questions.text: The magnitude of a vector A = 3î + 4ĵ is:
questions.explanation: sqrt(3² + 4²) = 5.

questions.text: The acceleration in uniform circular motion is called:
questions.explanation: It is the acceleration required to keep an object moving in a circle.

questions.text: Resolution of vectors means:
questions.explanation: Usually into mutually perpendicular components like x and y.

questions.text: If A·B = |AxB|, the angle between A and B is:
questions.explanation: cos θ = sin θ => tan θ = 1 => θ = 45°.

questions.text: The direction of angular velocity (ω) is along:
questions.explanation: Determined by the right-hand thumb rule.

questions.text: Projectile motion is a combination of:
options: Uniform horizontal velocity and constant vertical acceleration
Constant horizontal acceleration and uniform vertical velocity
questions.explanation: Gravity affects vertical motion; no force affects horizontal motion (ignoring air resistance).

questions.text: Relation between linear velocity (v) and angular velocity (ω) is:
questions.explanation: Linear velocity is the product of angular velocity and radius.

questions.text: The range of a projectile depends on:
questions.explanation: Formula: R = (u² sin 2θ)/g.

questions.text: If a boat can travel at 5m/s in still water and the river flows at 3m/s, the shortest time to cross is when it rows:
questions.explanation: Crossing time depends only on the component of velocity perpendicular to the banks.

questions.text: Two projectiles are fired at angles θ and (90-θ). Their horizontal ranges are:
questions.explanation: sin 2θ = sin 2(90-θ) = sin(180-2θ).

questions.text: Which law states that vectors can be added by placing the tail of one on the head of another?
questions.explanation: Triangle and Polygon laws use head-to-tail addition.

questions.text: A unit vector has a magnitude of:
questions.explanation: The primary purpose of a unit vector is to specify direction.

questions.text: Centripetal acceleration formula is:
questions.explanation: Derived from the geometry of circular motion.

questions.text: Angular displacement is measured in:
questions.explanation: Radian is the SI unit for plane angles.

questions.text: The vector product î x ĵ is:
questions.explanation: Cyclic order: î -> ĵ -> k̂.

questions.text: In non-uniform circular motion, net acceleration is the vector sum of:
questions.explanation: Centripetal changes direction; Tangential changes speed.

questions.text: If the angle between A and B is 180°, their resultant is:
questions.explanation: The vectors are anti-parallel.

questions.text: Which of the following remains constant in projectile motion (no air resistance)?
questions.explanation: Horizontal velocity u cos θ remains constant as there is no horizontal force.

questions.text: Angle between velocity and acceleration in uniform circular motion is:
questions.explanation: Velocity is tangential, acceleration is radial.

questions.text: The dot product of î and ĵ is:
questions.explanation: Orthogonal vectors have zero dot product.

questions.text: For two vectors A and B, A + B = B + A represents:
questions.explanation: Vector addition is commutative.

questions.text: Frequency (f) of circular motion is related to time period (T) as:
questions.explanation: Frequency is the reciprocal of the time period.

questions.text: The maximum range of a projectile is 400m. The maximum height attained will be:
questions.explanation: For θ=45°, R_max = u²/g. H = (u² sin² 45)/2g = u²/(4g) = R_max / 4.

questions.text: Displacement is always:
questions.explanation: Displacement depends only on initial and final positions.

questions.text: The unit of angular acceleration (α) is:
questions.explanation: Rate of change of angular velocity.
